provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Inquests (Expenses and Fees) Regulations.

provisions.title: Payment of Coroners
provisions.content: Nothing in these Regulations shall apply in respect of any payment for the services of Coroners.

provisions.title: Payment of winesses
provisions.content: Every person summoned to attend as a witness in proceedings before a Coroners' Court shall be entitled to be paid his travelling expenses and allowances for accommodation and subsistence in accordance with the provisions of the Criminal Proceedings (Expenses of Assessors and Witnesses) Rules 1 . 1 G.N. No. 352 of 1964

provisions.title: Payment of medical practitioners
provisions.content: A medical practitioner, other than a Government Medical Officer, having duties to perform in respect of matters under the Inquests Act, other than the adduction of evidence at an inquest, shall be paid fees 2 for the following items: 2 Fees omitted: Subject to variation. (a) carrying out an external inspection on the order of a Coroner or a police officer; (b) carrying out a complete postmortem examination; (c) superintending exhumation of body.

provisions.title: Fees include making of report
provisions.content: The fees specified in paragraph 4 shall include the making of a prescribed report to the Coroner arising from the carrying out of the duty in question.

provisions.title: Fee for copy of documents
provisions.content: Every person applying for a copy of the following documents, that is to say— (a) report of post-mortem examination; or (b) a copy of depositions taken by a Coroner at any inquest; or (c) a copy of any document put and received in evidence at any inquest, shall pay a fee 3 . 3 Fees omitted: Subject to variation.
